creator: dada@pluto.tugraz.at
text:
OK, here is a fresh devel/autoconf-archive port that replaces the broken
devel/ac-archive. (It could even coexist, because the patch makes it adhere
to BSD's hier(7) rather than the GNUish directories.)

For those confused by the autoconf-archive vs. ac-archive similarity:
The autoconf-archive port submitted here tracks autoconf-archive.cryp.to
which is the original project. The broken ac-archive port tracks a project
clone at ac-archive.sourceforge.net which mirrors the macros from the primary
project and adds Linuxisms such as scrollkeeper integration and RPM building.
Sourceforge's ac-archive seems badly maintained; from Dec. 2005 to October 2006
the then latest distribution was completely broken (the distribution tarball
lacked the macros themselves).
NB.: The ac-archive project's maintainer consistently forgets to give credit
to the primary project, which is enough reason for me not to do any
maintenance work for the FreeBSD port.

The shell archive below contains the port of the primary project.
Resolving the issue of this PR, i.e. removal (or marking as BROKEN)
the devel/ac-archive port is up to the committer.

HTH,
 Martin

# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	devel
#	devel/autoconf-archive
#	devel/autoconf-archive/files
#	devel/autoconf-archive/files/patch-Makefile.in
#	devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-descr
#	devel/autoconf-archive/distinfo
#	devel/autoconf-archive/Makefile
#	devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-plist
#
echo c - devel
mkdir -p devel > /dev/null 2>&1
echo c - devel/autoconf-archive
mkdir -p devel/autoconf-archive > /dev/null 2>&1
echo c - devel/autoconf-archive/files
mkdir -p devel/autoconf-archive/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - devel/autoconf-archive/files/patch-Makefile.in
sed 's/^X//' >devel/autoconf-archive/files/patch-Makefile.in << 'END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/files/patch-Makefile.in'
X--- Makefile.in.orig	Wed Oct 18 15:31:05 2006
X+++ Makefile.in		Wed Oct 18 15:31:16 2006
X@@ -24,7 +24,7 @@
X srcdir = @srcdir@
X top_srcdir = @top_srcdir@
X VPATH = @srcdir@
X-pkgdatadir = $(datadir)/@PACKAGE@
X+pkgdatadir = $(datadir)/doc/@PACKAGE@
X pkglibdir = $(libdir)/@PACKAGE@
X pkgincludedir = $(includedir)/@PACKAGE@
X top_builddir = .
X@@ -139,7 +139,7 @@
X sysconfdir = @sysconfdir@
X target_alias = @target_alias@
X dist_pkgdata_DATA = AUTHORS COPYING README
X-aclocaldir = $(datadir)/aclocal
X+aclocaldir = $(datadir)/@PACKAGE@
X dist_aclocal_DATA = $(M4SOURCE)
X htmldir = $(pkgdatadir)/html
X dist_html_DATA = $(HTMLDOC)
END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/files/patch-Makefile.in
echo x - devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-descr << 'END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-descr'
XThe Autoconf Macro Archive aims to provide a collection of reusable
XAutoconf macros as free software. The archive currently features more
Xthan 300 macros which perform portability tests ranging from compiler
Xsupport for weird language extensions to automatic generation of
Xsophisticated Automake rules.  However, all these macros have been
Xcontributed by friendly supporters of the cause from all over the
XInternet; the archive really just collects and distributes them.
X
XWWW: http://autoconf-archive.cryp.to/
END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-descr
echo x - devel/autoconf-archive/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >devel/autoconf-archive/distinfo << 'END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/distinfo'
XMD5 (autoconf-archive-2006-10-13.tar.bz2) = 0d6e117b4ed9f39dacd53abc27abbccf
XSHA256 (autoconf-archive-2006-10-13.tar.bz2) = 8ff082da6cabbd64d861932addb3895ad544069a185dda8ef58f518f30883bec
XSIZE (autoconf-archive-2006-10-13.tar.bz2) = 488235
END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/distinfo
echo x - devel/autoconf-archive/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >devel/autoconf-archive/Makefile << 'END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for: autoconf-archive
X# Date Created:	18 October 2006
X# Whom:		Martin Kammerhofer
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	autoconf-archive
XDISTVERSION=	2006-10-13
XCATEGORIES=	devel
XMASTER_SITES=	http://autoconf-archive.cryp.to/ \
X		http://mitglied.lycos.de/mkamm/distfiles/
X
XMAINTAINER=	mkamm@gmx.net
XCOMMENT=	A set of useful GNU Autoconf macros
X
XUSE_BZIP2=	yes
XUSE_AUTOTOOLS=	autoconf:259
XGNU_CONFIGURE=	yes
XCONFIGURE_TARGET= --build=${MACHINE_ARCH}-portbld-freebsd${OSREL}
X
X.if defined(NOPORTDOCS)
Xdo-install:
X	cd ${WRKSRC} && ${MAKE} install-dist_aclocalDATA
X.endif
X
X# next rule for maintainer use only: automatically create pkg-plist
Xplist:
X	@test -e ${CONFIGURE_COOKIE} || { \
X		${ECHO_MSG} >&2 "do 'make configure' first!"; exit 64; }
X	cd ${WRKSRC} && ${MAKE} -V DATA | \
X	${TR} ' ' '\n' | \
X	${SED}	-e 's,^./m4src/,%%DATADIR%%/,' \
X		-e 's,^./,%%PORTDOCS%%%%DOCSDIR%%/,' \
X		-e 's,/htmldoc/,/html/,' \
X		-e 's,^[A-Z],%%PORTDOCS%%%%DOCSDIR%%/&,' | \
X	${SORT} -u >${PLIST}
X	${ECHO_CMD} '%%PORTDOCS%%@dirrm %%DOCSDIR%%/html' >>${PLIST}
X	${ECHO_CMD} '%%PORTDOCS%%@dirrm %%DOCSDIR%%' >>${PLIST}
X	${ECHO_CMD} '@dirrm %%DATADIR%%' >>${PLIST}
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/Makefile
echo x - devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-plist << 'END-of-devel/autoconf-archive/pkg-plist'
